## Process Agent Shortcuts (AI-PILC Generated)

The following shortcut invokes the AI-PILC governance agent. Type the shortcut anywhere in a chat prompt — the corresponding agent activates immediately. No further clarification needed.

**How it works:** The shortcut is a direct command. When the AI sees it in your prompt, it loads the corresponding agent from `.kiro/agents/` and executes its full check pass.

### Active Shortcuts

| Shortcut | Agent | When to Call | Tier |
|----------|-------|-------------|:----:|
| `IQA__` | `initiation-quality-agent` — Initiation Quality Assessment | After PIP completion, before AI-ADLC handoff | 1 |

### Shortcut Rules

1. **`IQA__` triggers Initiation Quality Assessment.** When the user types `IQA__` anywhere in a prompt, invoke `.kiro/agents/initiation-quality-agent.md` and execute its full check pass. No clarification needed — treat `IQA__` as a direct command to run the agent.

### When to Call

Call `IQA__` at these process milestones:

| Milestone | Why |
|-----------|-----|
| After Stage 16 (Package Assembly) | Validates the complete PIP before handoff |
| After PLANNING phase (Stage 14) | Mid-workflow checkpoint for complex projects |
| Before Steering Committee presentation | Ensures package meets governance standards |
| Before AI-ADLC handoff | Confirms `pilc-state.md` and artifacts are AI-ADLC-ready |

### What Happens If You Don't Call It

The PIP may contain:
- Missing deliverables (incomplete package)
- Unlogged decisions (broken audit trail)
- Stakeholder gaps (governance holes discovered mid-project)
- Cross-reference breaks (documents referencing non-existent artifacts)

The compliance audit agent (AI-GCE) will eventually detect these gaps, but by then AI-ADLC may have already built architecture on incomplete foundations.

### Quick Guide

For full documentation on the initiation quality checks, what happens if you skip, and how to recover, see:

> **`.governance/AGENT-GUIDE.md`** — the combined process agent user manual (AI-PILC section).
